ops.text: fli (pypi "flights[mcp]") cannot price OKA at all: fli.models.airport builds Airport = Enum("Airport", AIRPORT_NAMES) and both NAH and OKA carry the value "Naha Airport", so Airport.OKA silently aliases Airport.NAH — every `fli flights HND OKA ...` request is sent to Google as HND→NAH (Naha/Tahuna, Sangihe Islands, Indonesia) and returns success with zero flights, no error. Decode side has the same hole: _AIRPORT_BY_CODE in fli/search/_decoders.py iterates the enum (aliases skipped), so even a corrected request fails row-parse with "Unknown airport IATA code 'OKA'". Workaround that produced real quotes: monkeypatch FlightSearchFilters.format to rewrite "NAH"→"OKA" in the formatted request and set _AIRPORT_BY_CODE["OKA"] = Airport.NAH before searching. Any duplicate airport display name in AIRPORT_NAMES has this failure mode. Also: Google returns zero itineraries once the departure date has fully elapsed in the origin timezone (JST runs ahead — a "today" Japan-domestic quote issued in a US evening is already past).
